text: Polarized training intensity distribution produces greater improvements in time-trial performance than threshold training distribution in endurance athletes, with a moderate effect size.
population: trained endurance athletes (VO2max >50 ml/kg/min, >2 years experience)

text: Polarized training distributes intensity predominantly in low and high zones while minimizing time in the moderate/threshold zone, as opposed to threshold training which concentrates work near lactate threshold.

The evidence base comparing polarized vs. threshold training is limited, with only 4 randomized controlled trials meeting inclusion criteria and study quality scores of 4-5 out of 10 on the PEDro Scale.
population: trained endurance athletes (VO2max >50 ml/kg/min, >2 years experience)

text: Polarized training may improve both internal performance markers (VO2max/VO2peak) and external performance markers (time-trial, time-to-exhaustion) compared to threshold training.
population: trained endurance athletes (VO2max >50 ml/kg/min, >2 years experience)

text: A VO2max/peak threshold of 50 ml/kg/min and more than 2 years of training experience are commonly used criteria to define endurance-trained athletes in polarized vs. threshold training research.
